dimensions.name: coverage
dimensions.evidence: `definition.yaml` declares 6 deliverables (`validate-definition-go`, `validate-ownership-go`, `blocker-taxonomy-schema`, `pi-validate-definition-handler`, `pi-ownership-warn-handler`, `claude-ownership-warn-parity`) and `plan.json` places each exactly once across Waves 1-3.

dimensions.name: feasibility
dimensions.evidence: Dependencies are ordered correctly (`blocker-taxonomy-schema` in Wave 1, D1/D2 in Wave 2, D4/D5/D6 in Wave 3), but same-wave file ownership overlaps violate the rule: Wave 2 shares `internal/cli/app.go`; Wave 3 shares `adapters/pi/furrow.ts`, `adapters/pi/furrow.test.ts`, and `.furrow/rows/pre-write-validation-go-first/parity-verification.md`.

dimensions.name: specificity
dimensions.evidence: Every `plan.json` assignment names a concrete specialist and at least one owned path/glob, e.g. `blocker-taxonomy-schema` -> `harness-engineer` with `schemas/blocker-taxonomy.yaml`, and both Pi deliverables -> `typescript-specialist` with explicit adapter files.

dimensions.name: research-grounding
dimensions.evidence: The row is grounded in cited research artifacts: `definition.yaml` points to `.furrow/rows/migration-state-of-the-union/research.md` §§8-9, and the stated architecture decisions map directly to `.furrow/rows/pre-write-validation-go-first/research.md` Q1-Q5 findings.
